name: Oriental Noodles with Chili and Cashews
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1. Heat the vegetable oil in a pan and add the chopped red chili, spring onion, and snow peas. Cook over low heat for 2-3 minutes.
2. Add the sesame oil, light soy sauce, malt vinegar, cashew paste, lime juice, and lime rind to the pan. Stir to combine.
3. Continue cooking over gentle heat for an additional 3-4 minutes until all ingredients are well incorporated.
1. To make the noodles, sift the flour and salt into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add the eggs. Mix gradually with a fork until combined.
2. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes). Cover and let stand for 30 minutes.
3. Divide the dough into 4 portions. Roll one portion to about 3mm thickness using a rolling pin or sharp knife. Cut into 5mm wide strips.
4. Repeat with the remaining portions of dough. Spread onto a dry tea towel and sprinkle with plain flour. Set aside.
5. Cook the pasta in large pan of boiling water until just tender, then drain.
Serve by spooning over the sauce and tossing to combine. Sprinkle shredded chili, cashews on top.
